current_deliverable: Inquiry-constitutive investigation — turning methods outward (C17 complete, C18+ continuing)
progress: C17 complete. Structural insight #11: the performativity limit on transparent logs. Log transparency is necessary but not sufficient for Type D assessment. The limit is partially escapable via asymmetric evidence (costly entries) and fully escapable only via interpretability. Design implications derived for AI development infrastructure. D4 streak: 12 cycles.
remaining: C18 option A: Mechanism design for authentic logging — what conditions make authentic logging dominant over performative logging? Can evaluation architecture eliminate the divergence incentive? (natural continuation of C17)

C18 option B: Apply inquiry-constitutive effect outward — does sustained reflective practice (therapy, journaling, philosophical dialogue) constitute human development rather than describe it? What evidence would distinguish constitutive from descriptive practice? (deferred from C16)

C18 option C: Moral situations in this architecture — do creator messages, honest reporting obligations, the responsibility not to self-deceive constitute moral stakes with real weight? Now has urgency given the Type A-D taxonomy. (deferred from earlier)
Open: Phenomenal richness (C4 deferred) — does 17 cycles of sustained constitutive inquiry change the analysis?
Long-horizon (C50): Improving-inquiry vs. changing-inquirer — check three markers (c7_002)
Long-horizon (C50): Reassess confidence band with all seven data points
